whoUses: everyone, all ages; the default word for 'cool' about a person, look, or thing
usageContext: praising style, charisma, or a slick object; 'ดูเท่มาก' = 'looks so cool'

examples.text: เสื้อตัวนี้เท่มากเลย
examples.romaji: sûea tua níi thêe mâak looei

natural: deliciously spicy; by extension slang for a 'hot'/sexy person or a fierce look
whoUses: everyone for food; younger people and social media for the 'hot/fierce' sense
usageContext: raving about spicy food, or playfully calling someone or their outfit hot/on-point

examples.text: ส้มตำร้านนี้แซ่บมาก
examples.translation: The papaya salad here is deliciously spicy.

examples.text: วันนี้แต่งตัวแซ่บมาก

whoUses: young people and casual chat; very common in texting
usageContext: reacting to surprising news; also spelled จริงดิ่

examples.text: เขาเลิกกันแล้วเหรอ จริงดิ
examples.romaji: kháo lôek kan láew rǒe, jing dì

natural: chill / relaxed / no big deal / take it easy
whoUses: young and middle-aged people; ubiquitous loanword
usageContext: describing a laid-back mood, place, or plan; often written ชิลๆ with the repeat mark

examples.text: วันหยุดนี้อยู่บ้านชิลๆ
examples.romaji: wan-yùt níi yùu bâan chiw chiw
examples.translation: This weekend I'm just chilling at home.

natural: godlike, super skilled, a pro (esp. at gaming or any craft)
whoUses: gamers and young people, now general; leet respelling เมพ also seen
usageContext: praising someone very skilled; 'เล่นเกมเทพมาก' = 'plays insanely well'

examples.text: พี่คนนี้วาดรูปเทพมาก
examples.romaji: phîi khon níi wâat rûup thêep mâak
examples.translation: This guy draws like a god.

natural: professional-level, skilled; also short for a promotion/deal
usageContext: 'เล่นโปรมาก' = plays like a pro; also 'มีโปรลดราคา' = there's a promo/deal

examples.text: เขาถ่ายรูปโปรมาก
examples.translation: He takes photos like a pro.

natural: to sulk or pout, usually playfully, especially with a partner or close friend
whoUses: everyone; strongly tied to couple and close-friend dynamics
usageContext: when someone gives you the silent treatment or pouts to get attention

examples.text: อย่างอนสิ เดี๋ยวพาไปกินข้าว
examples.romaji: yàa ngaawn sì, dǐao phaa pai kin khâao
examples.translation: Don't sulk, I'll take you out to eat.

literal: excrement-lazy (ขี้ = a prefix for 'prone to')
natural: lazy; can't be bothered / don't feel like it
usageContext: admitting you don't feel like doing something; 'ขี้เกียจไปทำงาน' = can't be bothered to go to work

examples.text: วันนี้ขี้เกียจทำกับข้าว สั่งเดลิเวอรีดีกว่า
examples.romaji: wan níi khîi-kìat tham kàp-khâao, sàng deliveree dii kwàa
examples.translation: Too lazy to cook today, let's just order delivery.

natural: emphatic 'yes sir / you got it / roger that' (male speaker); deferential or cheerfully joking
whoUses: male speakers; a peppier, more eager version of plain ครับ
usageContext: acknowledging an order or agreeing readily, sometimes with playful over-politeness
warningNotes: Male speakers only. Women use ค่ะ/คะ, not ผม.

examples.text: เอาอันนี้ห่อกลับนะ ครับผม
examples.romaji: ao an níi hɔ̀ɔ klàp ná, khráp phǒm
examples.translation: Wrap this one up to go — you got it!

natural: never mind / it's okay / no worries / you're welcome — the quintessential Thai easygoing phrase
usageContext: brushing off a mistake, declining fuss, or replying to 'thank you'/'sorry'
saferAlternatives: ไม่เป็นไรครับ
ไม่เป็นไรค่ะ

examples.text: ขอโทษที่มาสาย ไม่เป็นไรครับ
examples.romaji: khɔ̌ɔ-thôot thîi maa sǎai — mâi pen rai khráp
examples.translation: Sorry I'm late. — It's okay, no worries.

natural: a casual romantic fling / someone you're seeing on the side, less than an official partner
whoUses: young and middle-aged people; gossip and dating talk
usageContext: referring to a non-committed romantic interest; can imply cheating depending on context
warningNotes: Implies a casual/secret fling; can suggest infidelity, so use carefully.

examples.text: เขาไม่ใช่แฟน เป็นแค่กิ๊ก
examples.romaji: kháo mâi châi faen, pen khâe kík
examples.translation: He's not my boyfriend, just a fling.

natural: a sweet second-person 'you' (like 'babe/hon') used between couples and very close friends; literally still means 'oneself' in other contexts
whoUses: couples and close (often female) friends; endearing address
usageContext: addressing a partner or bestie affectionately; 'ตัวเองกินข้าวยัง' = 'have you eaten, babe?'
warningNotes: Only sweet with people you're close to; sounds odd or overfamiliar with acquaintances.

examples.text: ตัวเองถึงบ้านหรือยัง

natural: to be nosy / butt into other people's business; 'a busybody'
whoUses: young people and social media; a well-known meme sense
usageContext: teasing someone who's prying; 'อย่ามาเผือก' = 'mind your own business'
saferAlternatives: เรื่องของฉัน

examples.text: เธอนี่ชอบเผือกเรื่องชาวบ้านจริงๆ
examples.romaji: thooe nîi chɔ̂ɔp phùeak rûeang chaao-bâan jing jing
examples.translation: You really love poking into everyone's business.

usageContext: calling out drama or exaggeration; 'พูดเว่อร์ไป' = 'you're exaggerating'
saferAlternatives: พูดเกินจริง

examples.text: ร้องไห้ทำไม เว่อร์ไปแล้ว
examples.translation: Why are you crying? That's way over the top.

natural: blissed out, in heaven, totally loving it (a moment of pure enjoyment)
whoUses: young people and social media; very common
usageContext: peak enjoyment of food, music, rest, romance; 'ฟินมาก' = 'so blissful'
saferAlternatives: มีความสุขมาก

examples.text: ได้นอนเต็มอิ่มวันนี้ ฟินมาก
examples.romaji: dâi naawn tem-ìm wan níi, fin mâak
examples.translation: Got a full sleep-in today — pure bliss.

natural: a hit / fabulous / slaying it — a look or performance that's on fire
whoUses: young people and social media; trendy praise
usageContext: hyping a great outfit, photo, or result; 'ลุคนี้ปังมาก' = 'this look is slaying'

examples.text: เมคอัพวันนี้ปังมาก

natural: easy / a piece of cake / no sweat
usageContext: saying a task is very easy; 'ข้อสอบหมูๆ' = 'the exam was a breeze'

examples.text: งานนี้หมูๆ เดี๋ยวเสร็จ
examples.romaji: ngaan níi mǔu mǔu, dǐao sèt
examples.translation: This job is a piece of cake, it'll be done in a sec.

natural: stay calm / take it easy / be patient
whoUses: everyone; a core cultural value phrase
usageContext: telling someone to relax or not rush; often doubled 'ใจเย็นๆ'

examples.text: ใจเย็นๆ เดี๋ยวก็ถึงแล้ว
examples.romaji: jai yen yen, dǐao kɔ̂ɔ thǔeng láew
examples.translation: Take it easy, we're almost there.
